name: Ukraine
description: Ukraine is the 'breadbasket of Europe'. Russia's full-scale invasion in 2022 severely disrupted its agricultural exports.
risk_note: Active war zones. Black Sea port blockades. Farmland mining. 25% of farmland in conflict zones.

name: Russia
description: World's largest wheat exporter. Also dominant in fertilizer exports. Western sanctions create logistics disruptions.
risk_note: Under major Western sanctions. Circumventing via third countries. Fertilizer exports partially blocked.

name: Sudan
description: Civil war since April 2023 has devastated agricultural production and disrupted food supply chains.
risk_note: Ongoing civil war between SAF and RSF. Breadbasket regions affected.

name: Myanmar
description: Military coup in 2021 and ongoing insurgencies disrupt agricultural production and export logistics.
risk_note: Post-coup instability. Multiple armed conflicts. Currency collapse.

name: Ethiopia
description: Tigray conflict and ongoing regional instability affects agricultural exports and food security.
risk_note: Tigray ceasefire but fragile. Amhara conflict ongoing. Drought cycles.

name: Yemen
description: Long civil war has devastated Yemen's minimal agricultural exports. Country is primarily a food importer.
risk_note: Ongoing Houthi-Saudi conflict. Houthi Red Sea attacks affect regional imports.

name: United States
description: Largest global agricultural exporter. Stable supply but prices affected by global conflict-driven demand shifts.
risk_note: No active conflict. Stable exports. Climate risk from drought.

name: Brazil
description: Brazil has become an indispensable alternative supplier as conflict disrupts traditional Black Sea exporters.
risk_note: No conflict risk. Growing market share as Ukraine/Russia disrupted.

name: Australia
description: Major wheat and barley exporter. Benefits from demand shifts away from Black Sea suppliers.
risk_note: Stable. Climate risk (El Nino droughts). No conflict exposure.

name: Canada
description: Second largest wheat exporter. Canadian Prairie wheat quality is highly sought after by buyers avoiding Russian grain.
risk_note: No conflict risk. Weather-related production variability.
